title: Mischief Mouse and the Tiny Lego Hat
summary: Mischief Mouse returns to Hattie’s workshop because her custom Lego hat is broken and missing pieces. The two friends work diligently to rebuild the headpiece block by block, stacking many Legos together. However, when the project is finished, the hat is far too small and falls off Mischief's head. To manage her frustration, Mischief takes a deep breath and counts to four before explaining the problem. Undeterred, Hattie decides to dismantle the small hat and rebuild it even larger to ensure the perfect fit.

highlightQuote.text: Take a deep breath and count to four.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What should we tell a story about this time? Just a quick one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A Hattie story. Okay. Once upon a time, a customer came to Hattie's house with a question about a hat that Hattie had made. Who was the customer? Was it Lion or Octopus or Pooh Bear?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I can't remember. Oh! I know, I know, I know, I know! It was Mischief.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it was Mischief Mouse. And Hattie the mouse had made for Mischief Mouse a beautiful hat made of Legos. But Mischief Mouse came to Hattie and said 'There's a problem with my Lego hat. It got broken and part of it's missing, I'm not even sure what part it was.' And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mischief said, 'Can you help me please?'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'Yes, yes, yes, I can help you.' And so Mischief and Hattie decided to make a plan to fix the hat. So what was the first step of their plan?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Put another Lego on. Click. And Mischief said 'Oh, hmm, we're almost there. We need a few more Legos.' What's the next step?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And another Lego, and another Lego, and another Lego, and a lot of Legos.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A lot of Legos. And so Hattie and Mischief put a lot of Legos together and in that way they made a new Lego hat for Mischief. But then Mischief looked at the hat, tried to put it on her head and she noticed that it didn't fit and it fell right off. And she was mad. So what did she do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Take a deep breath and count to four.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Mischief took a deep breath and counted to four. One, two, three, four. And Hattie said 'What's the matter?' And what did Mischief say then?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's too small, says Hattie. Well, let's try again. We'll take apart the Lego hat we just built and add a few more parts and make it bigger and then I think it will fit just right. And so they did. Click click click they put all the Legos together.

characters.name: Hattie
characters.description: A clever mouse milliner and engineer of whimsy, she is a resourceful spirit who crafts exquisite hats and soulful portraits within her hollow tree stump workshop. Though a formidable presence famously counted among the thirty legendary cats, she is currently confined to her bed by illness, her usual treks through saturated woodlands for rare tape and lollipops temporarily halted. Even in her malaise, her sharp mind lingers on a recent, stinging disagreement with Dimitar over a fish, a rare shadow cast upon a heart otherwise known for its boundless generosity and creative light.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

places.name: Hattie's House
places.description: Nestled in a sun-drenched tree across from the town park, Hattie’s arboreal sanctuary on Mouse Street doubles as a vibrant Cornell Street workshop for designing hats and a peaceful retreat for resting after long days of painting. This cozy home, cluttered with the tools of her trade, is a place of shared morning breakfasts with Mischief and savory meals cooked alongside her Nana. Whether lulled by the rhythmic drumming of rainfall or the quiet exhaustion of her artistry, Hattie finds her solace here, drifting into restorative naps amidst the creative clutter.
